settings.heading: Frequently Asked **Questions**
What is Nitric Oxide and why is it important?
settings.content:
Nitric Oxide is a signaling molecule produced in the body that helps:

  • Relax and dilate blood vessels
  • Regulate blood pressure
  • Improve circulation
  • Deliver oxygen and nutrients to tissues
  • Support mitochondrial energy production
  • Maintain endothelial function

Nitric Oxide production naturally declines with age.

settings.heading: Why does Nitric Oxide decline as we age?
settings.content:
Nitric Oxide declines due to:

  • Reduced endothelial nitric oxide synthase (eNOS) activity
  • Oxidative stress
  • Chronic inflammation
  • Insulin resistance
  • Hormonal changes
  • Poor oral microbiome health

By age 40–50, Nitric Oxide production may be significantly reduced compared to early adulthood.

settings.heading: How are Berkeley Life products different from other nitric oxide supplements?
settings.content:
Many Nitric Oxide supplements use L-arginine or L-citrulline.

Berkeley Life uses dietary nitrates, which:

  • Bypass age-related decline in the arginine pathway
  • Work independently of eNOS
  • Have strong clinical validation
  • Provide more consistent nitric oxide production

Our formulas are clinically studied and practitioner-trusted.

settings.heading: Is Berkeley Life clinically studied?
settings.content:
Yes. Berkeley Life has conducted double-blind, placebo-controlled clinical research evaluating dietary nitrate supplementation and nitric oxide levels.

Results demonstrated:

  • Increased salivary nitrite
  • Increased plasma nitrite
  • Improved nitric oxide bioavailability

Additional research continues in specific populations.

settings.heading: Are Berkeley Life products third-party tested?
settings.content:
Yes.

Every batch undergoes:

  • Potency verification
  • Heavy metal testing
  • Microbiological screening
  • Certificate of Analysis (CoA) review

Manufacturing follows cGMP (21 CFR Part 111) standards in the United States.

settings.heading: Where are Berkeley Life products made?
settings.content:
Our supplements are manufactured in the United States in FDA-registered, GMP-certified facilities.

Nitric Oxide Test Strips are manufactured in Europe.

settings.heading: Do Berkeley Life products contain hormones?
settings.content:
No.

Berkeley Life formulas are non-hormonal and do not contain estrogen, progesterone, or testosterone.

settings.heading: Are Berkeley Life products non-GMO?
settings.content: Yes. Our products are non-GMO.

settings.heading: Can I take Berkeley Life with prescription medications?
settings.content:
Because nitric oxide supports vasodilation, individuals taking:

  • Blood pressure medications
  • Anticoagulants
  • PDE5 inhibitors
  • Organic nitrates
  • SSRIs (for pollen-containing products)

should consult their healthcare provider before use.

settings.heading: Is it safe for people with oxalate sensitivities?
settings.content: Yes. The oxalate content is extremely low - less than a single spinach leaf. Even individuals with sensitivities generally tolerate it well.

settings.heading: Is potassium nitrate safe?
settings.content:
Berkeley Life uses food-grade potassium nitrate. It is chemically identical to the nitrate naturally found in leafy green vegetables.

It is not the same as industrial-grade nitrates used in fertilizers or explosives.

settings.heading: How long does it take to see results?
settings.content:
Many users report improvements in:

  • Circulation
  • Energy
  • Test strip readings

within 1–2 weeks.

Clinical vascular improvements are typically measured at 8–12 weeks.

Results vary by individual.

settings.heading: Can Nitric Oxide become harmful?
settings.content:
Nitric Oxide is a natural signaling molecule essential for vascular health.

In conditions of extreme oxidative stress, reactive nitrogen species may form. Maintaining a healthy diet and lifestyle helps support balanced nitric oxide activity.

Dietary nitrates from vegetables and Berkeley Life formulas are considered safe for daily use in healthy adults.

settings.heading: How do the Nitric Oxide Test Strips work?
settings.content:
The strips measure salivary nitrite using the Griess reaction.

Salivary nitrite is a validated indicator of nitric oxide pathway activity.

They provide a simple, at-home way to track nitric oxide levels in 15 seconds.

settings.heading: Why might my test strips stay low?
settings.content:
Low readings may be related to:

  • Use of antibacterial mouthwash
  • Recent antibiotic use
  • Oral microbiome imbalance
  • High oxidative stress
  • Genetic factors

Consistent daily supplementation may help improve readings over time.

settings.heading: Can I take Berkeley Life long-term?
settings.content:
Yes.

Nitric Oxide production naturally declines with age. Many individuals choose long-term use as part of their healthy aging routine.

Consult your healthcare provider for personalized guidance.

settings.heading: How long does shipping take in the United States?
settings.content:
U.S. orders typically arrive within:

6 business days from submission

  • Orders placed before 3pm PST are processed same day
  • Fulfillment within 24 hours
  • Shipping time: approximately 5 business days

settings.heading: Do you ship to Canada?
settings.content:
Yes.

Capsules may be ordered in 90-day increments per Health Canada regulations.

  • Shipping cost: $16.95
  • Delivery: 7–10 business days (may vary)

settings.heading: Why am I being charged sales tax?
settings.content:
Berkeley Life complies with state tax regulations, including Michigan sales and use tax laws.

Internet supplement purchases may be taxed differently than in-store purchases depending on state regulations.

settings.heading: How do subscriptions work?
settings.content:
Subscribers receive:

  • Automatic recurring shipments
  • Priority processing
  • Easy account management

Subscriptions can be managed or canceled anytime through your account dashboard.

settings.heading: Can I remove capsules from the blister pack?
settings.content:
Yes.

You may remove up to a week’s worth for pill organizers. Mild discoloration may occur but does not affect potency.

settings.heading: I have a different question.
settings.content: If you have a question that hasn’t been answered here, please contact our customer support team via https://berkeleylife.com/pages/contact We’re happy to help.
